title: Ralph
description: Ralph is an autonomous one-story-per-iteration execution loop driven by a product requirements backlog.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Ralph is an autonomous execution technique that works through a product requirements backlog one user story per iteration. A product requirements document (PRD) backlog such as prd.json, plus progress notes, drives which story comes next. Ralph is not the factory itself and not a one-shot chat reply.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Long product requirements stay mergeable and reviewable because each iteration finishes one small story, carries learnings forward, and avoids stuffing an entire feature into one context window.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Read the PRD backlog, pick the highest-priority unfinished story, implement and verify that story, mark it complete, record progress and learnings, then continue until stories pass or an explicit stop path ends the run.

comparedToNearbyTechniques.title: Compared To Nearby Techniques
comparedToNearbyTechniques.body: Ralph is not a plain factory loop that only keeps iterating until a goal is done. It is also not planner-executor (plan then execute as a role split), writer-reviewer (draft plus an explicit review gate), or workqueue-executor (drain a work queue).
